commands.extension.full-userbuild: Run IBM User Build with full upload
description: Provides COBOL, PL/I, HLASM, and REXX language servers, as well as tools for IBM Z development.
preprocessor.local-mvs.execute: Execute remote preprocessor command for local file
preprocessor.local.diff.check: Compare local preprocessor input and output files
preprocessor.mvs.diff.check: Compare remote preprocessor input and output files
snippets.importidzsnippet: Import code snippets from IBM Developer for z/OS
userSettings.dbbDefaultZappProfile: Name of the ZAPP profile to be used when running a User Build
userSettings.dbbLogDir: Remote z/OS directory where logs for the user build will be written
userSettings.dbbWorkspace: Absolute path for the remote location of your personal DBB workspace where your programs will be copied for the user build
userSettings.dbb_hlq: TSO high-level qualifier to be used for the user build
userSettings.description: User-specific settings for User Build. Add these settings to your VS Code User Settings as these settings will be different for every user such as HLQ.
userSettings.localLogDir: Local directory where User Build logs will be downloaded. Path can be absolute or relative to the workspace of the build file.
userSettings.title: User Settings for IBM User Build
wazideploy.evidence.dataFolder: The location of the existing Wazi Deploy evidence on the remote z/OS
wazideploy.evidence.description: In this section, you can define the Wazi Deploy MCP user settings
wazideploy.evidence.excecutablePath: Full path to the wazideploy-evidence executable (optional)
wazideploy.evidence.indexFolder: The location of the Wazi Deploy generated indexes on the remote z/OS
wazideploy.evidence.title: Wazi Deploy MCP User Settings
workspaceSettings.title: Workspace Settings for IBM User Build
zopeneditor.JAVA_HOME: To override the default Java used by Z Open Editor, point to a 64-Bit Java. The file path must exclude the "/bin/java" suffix. A sample file path on MacOS is "/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/ibm-semeru-open-21.jdk"
zopeneditor.advancedCapabilities.enableR: Enable launch 3270 emulator action in an external browser
Enable launch 3270 emulator action in VS Code
Enable automatic activation key checks at startup using your default RSE API Zowe profile. (When disabled a check will be performed when you actively connect to RSE API.)
zopeneditor.autoPreprocessor: Specify when the COBOL preprocessor is invoked
zopeneditor.autoPreprocessor.alldesc: Run automatically when file is either opened, receives focus, or saved
zopeneditor.autoPreprocessor.focusdesc: Run automatically when file's editor receives the focus
zopeneditor.autoPreprocessor.opendesc: Run automatically when file is opened
zopeneditor.autoPreprocessor.savedesc: Run automatically when file is saved
zopeneditor.cobol.addComment: Adds custom keybinding for 'CtrlCmd+/' to perform a single line comment as well as multi-line block comment based on code selection in the COBOL Editor.
zopeneditor.cobol.disableProblems: Disable code problems in the Problems view and editor. It will keep other language features such as Outline view available if possible. It will also disable unreachable code warnings. The default value is false.
zopeneditor.cobol.enableLiveZCodeScan.de: Enables IBM ZCodeScan to run in the background while editing a file. When disabled, you can manually run 'Execute IBM ZCodeScan' from the command palette or context menu. Disabling is useful if you want to temporarily turn off static code analysis to reduce noise in editor and log files as well as improve performance during large edits.
zopeneditor.cobol.enableUnreachableCodeW: Enable unreachable code warnings. Unreachable code in a program is code that can never be executed because there is no path to the code from the rest of the program. This setting is enabled by default.
zopeneditor.cobol.includeFileExtensions: Specify the file extensions to use for finding COBOL include files on your local file system. To edit an include file locally, a file extension is required.
zopeneditor.cobol.maximumLineLength: Specify the maximum line length for the COBOL Editor. If specified, all text after the provided column number will be truncated when you save a file. To disable truncation of characters, set the maximum line length to -1.
zopeneditor.cobol.sequenceNumbers: Choose which columns will have sequence numbers inserted and removed from a file by using the Renumber and Unnumber functions in the COBOL editor.
zopeneditor.cobol.tabstops: Set tab stop locations to specify where the cursor stops when you press the Tab key.
zopeneditor.cobol.truncate: Check for COBOL truncation and save
zopeneditor.datasets.cobolDatasets: MVS data set members do not have file extensions that are crucial for this editor to identify the language of the file being opened. If you access COBOL programs from MVS, you can provide the data set names that contain COBOL programs so that its members will be opened as COBOL and not text files in the editor. Note that entries in this list will update the file.associations setting. To remove a mapping, remove it here first and then in the file.associations setting.
zopeneditor.datasets.hlasmDatasets: MVS data set members do not have file extensions that are crucial for this editor to identify the language of the file being opened. If you access HLASM programs from MVS, you can provide the data set names that contain HLASM programs so that its members will be opened as HLASM and not text files in the editor. Note that entries in this list will update the file.associations setting. To remove a mapping, remove it here first and then in the file.associations setting.
zopeneditor.datasets.jclDatasets: MVS data set members do not have file extensions that are crucial for this editor to identify the language of the file being opened. If you access JCL programs from MVS, you can provide the data set names that contain JCL programs so that its members will be opened as JCL and not text files in the editor. Note that entries in this list will update the file.associations setting. To remove a mapping, remove it here first and then in the file.associations setting.
zopeneditor.datasets.pl1Datasets: MVS data set members do not have file extensions that are crucial for this editor to identify the language of the file being opened. If you access PL/I programs from MVS, you can provide the data set names that contain PL/I programs so that its members will be opened as PL/I and not text files in the editor. Note that entries in this list will update the file.associations setting. To remove a mapping, remove it here first and then in the file.associations setting.
zopeneditor.datasets.rexxDatasets: MVS data set members do not have file extensions that are crucial for this editor to identify the language of the file being opened. If you access REXX programs from MVS, you can provide the data set names that contain REXX programs so that its members will be opened as REXX and not text files in the editor. Note that entries in this list will update the file.associations setting. To remove a mapping, remove it here first and then in the file.associations setting.
zopeneditor.disableProblems: Disable code problems in the Problems view and editor. It will keep other language features such as Outline view available if possible. The default value is false.
zopeneditor.enableCICSCodeLens: Enable CodeLens for files containing EXEC CICS statements. The default value is true.
zopeneditor.enableCodeFolding: Enable code folding support for this language. The default value is true.
zopeneditor.encodings.filePatterns.descr:
Associate file patterns with an encoding. If a file that matches a pattern is opened, it will use the provided encoding when evaluating the file for errors.
Note: This setting only controls what encodings are passed to the language servers. To control encodings used for file conversions when uploading or downloading z/OS resources, use your ZAPP file or Zowe profile.
These patterns should be specified as glob patterns. A single asterisk * matches to any number - including none - of characters within a path segment. A double asterisk ** matches to any number - including none - of path segments.

For example, `{ **/COBOL/*.cbl : IBM-930 }` will assign any file paths containing a directory named `COBOL` ending with a `.cbl` extension an encoding of `IBM-930`.

[Learn more about glob patterns.](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/editor/glob-patterns)
zopeneditor.encodings.filePatterns.title: Encodings for opened files.
zopeneditor.enforceCaseSensitiveIncludeF: To avoid ambiguous matching on case-insensitive file systems, you can enforce case-sensitive matching of local copybooks, include file names, and folder names in property groups. The default is False to match files with all cases in the path.
zopeneditor.hlasm.enableCustomMacrosWarn: Enable warnings for custom macros that are not found in the MACLIBs specified in your ZAPP file. When enabled, an underline will appear under macros that could not be located.
zopeneditor.hlasm.enableResolvingMacros: Enable automatic fetch of macros from the SYS1.MACLIB data set on MVS. When enabled, remote macros in SYS1.MACLIB are available for code completion and for preview in rich hovers, and will be downloaded for use as needed. To use this function, a valid Zowe Explorer connection is required to retrieve content from MVS.
zopeneditor.hlasm.includeFileExtensions: Specify the file extensions to be used for finding HLASM include files on your local file system. To edit an include file locally, a file extension is required.
zopeneditor.hlasm.instructionsFilePath: To override the default instructions specification, specify the absolute path to a new HLASM instructions JSON file.
zopeneditor.hlasm.macrosFilePath: To override the default macro index file that is included with the editor, specify the absolute path to an HLASM macros definition JSON file that is provided by IBM.
zopeneditor.hlasm.maximumLineLength: Specify the maximum line length for the HLASM Editor. If specified, all text after the provided column number will be truncated when you save a file. To disable truncation of characters, set the maximum line length to -1.
zopeneditor.hlasm.reduceCustomMacroLoggi: Enable reduced logging to enhance custom macro resolution performance. When enabled, the Z Open Editor log will not get display information about which macros are being requested from the client, but performance will be improved as the HLASM language server will be handling resolution without client communication.
zopeneditor.hlasm.remoteMaclibScanTimeou: Timeout value in milliseconds for scanning remote MACLIBs on MVS for custom macros. Increase this value if you have a large MACLIB and experience timeout issues when resolving custom macros.
zopeneditor.hlasm.tabstops: Set tab stop locations to specify where the cursor stops when you press the Tab key
zopeneditor.hlasm.truncate: Check for HLASM truncation and save
zopeneditor.hlasm.zosMacrosVersion: Choose which version of z/OS your host system is running to resolve HLASM Macros
zopeneditor.hlasm.zosMacrosVersion.v2r5: Macros host system is running z/OS 2.5
zopeneditor.hlasm.zosMacrosVersion.v3r1: Macros host system is running z/OS 3.1
zopeneditor.hlasm.zosMacrosVersion.v3r2: Macros host system is running z/OS 3.2
zopeneditor.jcl.enableResolvingProcs: Enable automatic resolving of procs in SYS1.PROCLIB. To use this function, a valid Zowe Explorer connection is required to retrieve content from MVS. SYS1.PROCLIB is searched only if an MVS PROCLIB property group is present in the ZAPP file.
zopeneditor.jcl.includeFileExtensions: Specify the file extensions to be used for finding JCL include files on your local file system. To edit an include file locally, a file extension is required.
zopeneditor.jcl.maximumLineLength: Specify the maximum line length for the JCL Editor. If specified, all text after the provided column number will be truncated when you save a file. To disable truncation of characters, set the maximum line length to -1.
zopeneditor.jcl.tabstops: Set tab stop locations to specify where the cursor stops when you press the Tab key
zopeneditor.jcl.truncate: Check for JCL truncation and save
zopeneditor.logger: Select the log level for IBM Z Open Editor. The default is INFO, which only logs important messages, warnings, and errors.
zopeneditor.mcp.enabled: Enable the Z Open Editor Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. You need to create a '.vscode/mcp.json' file in the workspace in which the server should be active. See the details in the Z Open Editor documentation.
zopeneditor.pl1.includeFileExtensions: Specify the file extensions to be used for finding PL/I include files on your local file system. To edit an include file locally, a file extension is required.
zopeneditor.pl1.maximumLineLength: Specify the maximum line length for the PL/I Editor. If specified, all text after the provided column number will be truncated when you save a file. To disable truncation of characters, set the maximum line length to -1.
zopeneditor.pl1.truncate: Check for PL/I truncation and save
zopeneditor.rexx.includeFileExtensions: Specify the file extensions to be used for finding REXX include files on your local file system. To edit an include file locally, a file extension is required.
zopeneditor.rexx.maximumLineLength: Specify the maximum line length for the REXX Editor. If specified, all text after the provided column number will be truncated when you save a file. To disable truncation of characters, set the maximum line length to -1.
zopeneditor.rexx.truncate: Check for REXX truncation and save
zopeneditor.server.memoryAllocation: Configure the maximum memory allocation pool for the Java virtual machine. Each language server will use this amount of memory (in MB) for each enterprise language. The value will be used for a -Xmx Java startup parameter. If omitted, the default value 512 applies. Specify another value if you are working with very large program files and require more memory, or you are on machines with not enough memory. Example: 1024
zopeneditor.server.startupOptions: Configure additional startup parameters for Java virtual machines started for running language servers. These might have been provided by tech support for troubleshooting. Leave blank by default.
zopeneditor.userbuild.alwaysShowCommands: (Experimental) Show User Build commands in context menus and the command palette for all files (excluding include files). By default, Z Open Editor will only display User Build commands for files with a COBOL, PL/I, or HLASM file extension.
zopeneditor.userbuild.enabled: Enable User Build capabilities that utilize IBM Dependency Based Build. When enabled, you can run a user build to compile and link programs during coding and unit testing simply by right-clicking inside a COBOL, PL/I, or HLASM file.
zopeneditor.welcomepage.show: Show the welcome page on VS Code startup
zopeneditor.zapp.customZappSchemaLocatio: When you work offline but have to provide a path to the ZAPP schema yourself, such as in a custom Che deployment, specify a valid HTTP or file URL to the location of the ZAPP JSON schema. If specified, the Use Default Online Zapp Schema option will be ignored.
zopeneditor.zapp.generateZappOnDemand: Automatically generate a generic ZAPP file when opening a COBOL, PL/I, HLASM, REXX program and none can be found in all the open workspaces. This file will search for include files in all workspaces and folders.
zopeneditor.zapp.useDefaultOnlineZappSch: Z Open Editor comes with the built-in ZAPP JSON schema. It can be used for YAML files using Red Hat's YAML VS Code extension, and for JSON files with the built-in editor. If you encounter issues locating this built-in schema on deployments like Eclipse Che, such as being unable to see syntax errors with the Red Hat YAML extension, enable this option to load the schema from the internet.
zopeneditor.zappVariables: Use to specify variable names with values that will be substituted in your ZAPP files. To reference a variable defined here in a ZAPP file write `${variable-name}`. For example, define a variable here called `HLQ` with a value that represents your personal high level qualifier on z/OS MVS. In your ZAPP file reference it in a property group's location entry like this: `${HLQ}.COBOL.COPYBOOKs`. Note, that all users need to define all the variables in their user settings that are used by the ZAPP file to avoid errors. Make sure to not use single or double-quote characters. You can place these properties in VS Code user as well as workspace settings.
zopeneditor.zcodeformat.customZCodeForma: When you work offline but have to provide a path to the zCodeFormat schema yourself, such as in a custom Che deployment, specify a valid HTTP or file URL to the location of the zCodeFormat JSON schema. If specified, the Use Default Online zCodeFormat Schema option will be ignored.
zopeneditor.zcodeformat.useDefaultOnline: Z Open Editor comes with the built-in zCodeFormat JSON schema. It can be used for YAML files using Red Hat's YAML VS Code extension, and for JSON files with the built-in editor. If you encounter issues locating this built-in schema on deployments like Eclipse Che, such as being unable to see syntax errors with the Red Hat YAML extension, enable this option to load the schema from the internet.
zopeneditor.zcodescan.enableLiveZCodeSca: Enables IBM ZCodeScan to run in the background while editing a file. When disabled, you can manually run 'Execute IBM ZCodeScan' from the command palette or context menu. Disabling is useful if you want to temporarily turn off static code analysis to reduce noise in editor and log files as well as improve performance during large edits.
zopeneditor.zowe: Specify default options for using Zowe CLI profiles and mapping file locations for various Z Open Editor operations such as loading include files from MVS using property groups. Typically you can specify these settings in your user settings using your personal profile name. If defined in workspace settings, it is assumed that all users of this workspace defined the same standardized profile names on their systems.
zopeneditor.zowe.defaultCliProfile: Provide the name of the Zowe CLI profile used for interacting with z/OS such as include file resolution by the language servers. It can be of type 'zosmf' or 'rse'. If no valid profile name is provided, the default RSE profile will be used; if not found, then the default z/OSMF profile will be used.
zopeneditor.zowe.defaultRseConversionMap: Either an absolute or workspace-relative path to a valid RSE API mapping file or ZAPP file that contains RSE API mapping. These mappings will be used for reading and writing to Zowe Explorer MVS files for RSE API profiles. Note that the property groups for remote include file resolution and user build will not use this setting, but a workspace-local ZAPP file; if not found, a mapping file in the user's .zowe/profiles/rse directory will be used.
zopeneditor.zowe.defaultSshCliProfile: Provide the name of the Zowe CLI SSH profile used for remote execution of commands on USS of your z/OS system. If no valid profile name is provided, the default SSH profile will be used.
zopeneditor.zowe.defaultTsoProfile: Provide the name of the Zowe TSO profile used for interacting with z/OS such as remote preprocessor execution. If no valid profile name is provided, the default TSO profile will be used.
zopeneditor.zowe.disableRseApiLanguageDe: Disable the advanced feature of language detection via RSE API for dataset members without extensions when opened under an RSE profile.
zopeneditor.zowe.listBeforeDownload: Control how Z Open Editor should download include files from z/OS data sets when using z/OSMF Zowe profiles. This setting has no effect when use RSE API profiles. Enable this setting to let the editor perform a list operation first to test if a file exists. This will require two z/OS requests for downloading each file, which will have a significant performance impact, but avoids causing Not Found messages in z/OS logs. Default is false.
zopeneditor.zowe.maximumParallelFileDown: Control how many include files Z Open Editor will try to download in parallel as a maximum from remote locations using your default Zowe CLI profile. Some technologies such as z/OSMF will require more resources for parallel downloads. Setting this value to 1 means sequential downloading. Default value is 5. Maximum is 50.
zopeneditor.zowe.maximumParallelFileUplo: Control how many files Z Open Editor will try to upload in parallel as a maximum from remote locations using your default Zowe CLI profile. This settings is currently utilized by the User Build feature. Some technologies such as z/OSMF will require more resources for parallel uploads. Setting this value to 1 means sequential uploads. Default value is 5. Maximum is 50.
zopeneditor.zowe.readonlyRemoteIncludeFi: Control whether remote include files opened from COBOL COPY or PL/I %INCLUDE statments are opened in readonly mode. This prevents saved edits to the file being written back to MVS on z/OS.
